adapter_contract.notes: Full beginner grammar lesson 02 preview with corrected JSON syntax and verified content integrity (P1 situational practice + P2 annotated flashcards).

title_i18n.zh_tw: Beginner Grammar Lesson 02 · 三大核心助詞 (은/는, 이/가, 을/를)
title_i18n.en: Beginner Grammar Lesson 02 · The Three Core Particles (은/는, 이/가, 을/를)

theme_i18n.zh_tw: 誰在說話、誰在做事、動作的對象
theme_i18n.en: Who is talking, who is doing, and the object of action

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 你能區分 은/는 和 이/가 的使用場景 (主題 vs 主語)。
can_do_i18n.en: Distinguish between 은/는 and 이/가 (Topic vs Subject).

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 你能正確在受詞後加上 을/를。
can_do_i18n.en: Correctly apply the object markers 을/를.

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 你能區分『我是學生』(一般介紹) 和『我才是學生』(指名主角) 的差別。
can_do_i18n.en: Tell the difference between 'I am a student' (intro) and 'I (am the one who) am a student' (focus).

can_do_i18n.zh_tw: 你能正確造出包含主語、受詞、動詞的簡單動作句。
can_do_i18n.en: Build basic sentence structures including subject, object, and verb.

title_i18n.zh_tw: L1 聽聽看：這些助詞怎麼跑出來的
title_i18n.en: L1 Listening: How these particles appear

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 先聽兩段對話。注意『我』在不同情境下的說法，以及對比時助詞怎麼變。
summary_i18n.en: Listen to two short dialogues. Notice how 'I' changes in different contexts.

what_to_notice_i18n.zh_tw: 注意主語助詞 (이/가) 和主題助詞 (은/는) 在指名對象與介紹話題時的差異。
what_to_notice_i18n.en: Notice the difference between Subject markers (이/가) and Topic markers (은/는) when spotlighting subjects vs. introducing topics.

what_to_notice_i18n.zh_tw: 注意受詞助詞 (을/를) 如何標示動作對象，以及換成 (은/는) 時產生的對比語感。
what_to_notice_i18n.en: Notice how Object markers (을/를) mark action targets, and how switching to (은/는) creates a sense of contrast.

title_i18n.en: Who is the student? (Subject vs Topic)

note_i18n.zh_tw: 這裡展現了『是我 (제가)』指名主角，與『至於我 (저는)』介紹話題的對比。
note_i18n.en: This shows the contrast between 'It's me (제가)' for spotlighting and 'As for me (저는)' for intro.

translations_i18n.en: Yuna, who is a student here?

translations_i18n.zh_tw: 啊，是嗎？（至於）我，我是老師。
translations_i18n.en: Oh, really? As for me, I am a teacher.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 注意『飯 (밥을)』作為單純受詞，與『不吃飯 (밥은 안 먹어요)』作為對比主題的變化。
note_i18n.en: Note the change from 'Meal (밥을)' as a direct object to 'Not eating rice (밥은...)' as a contrastive topic.

translations_i18n.en: Minsu, are you eating a meal?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: 아니요, 밥은 안 먹어요. 빵을 먹어요.

translations_i18n.zh_tw: 不，（至於）飯我不吃。我在吃麵包。
translations_i18n.en: No, as for rice, I'm not eating it. I'm eating bread.

expected_output_i18n.zh_tw: 能聽出『是我 (제가)』和『一般的我 (저는)』在語感上的差別。
expected_output_i18n.en: Recognize the nuance between 'Subject Focus Me' (제가) and 'Topic Intro Me' (저는).

title_i18n.zh_tw: L2 核心操作：主角 (이/가) vs 話題 (은/는)
title_i18n.en: L2 Core Lab: Subject (이/가) vs Topic (은/는)

summary_i18n.zh_tw: 練習切換不同的主體與焦點，看助詞如何根據收音和情境自動變身。
summary_i18n.en: Practice switching subjects and focus types. Watch how particles change based on batchim and context.

explanation_i18n.zh_tw: 一般自我介紹或話題起始。
explanation_i18n.en: Common self-intro or starting a topic.

explanation_i18n.zh_tw: 指名主角身分，回答『誰是...』。

title_i18n.zh_tw: L3 進階操作：受詞 (을/를) vs 對比 (은/稱)

explanation_i18n.zh_tw: 單純標示動作的目標受詞。
explanation_i18n.en: Simple object marking for the action.

explanation_i18n.zh_tw: 產生強烈的對比感，暗示別的不吃。
explanation_i18n.en: Creates strong contrast, implying other things aren't eaten.

title_i18n.zh_tw: G1 文法全景：三大助詞總結
title_i18n.en: G1 Grammar Hub: The Particle Trio

title_i18n.zh_tw: P1 組句挑戰：核心助詞大會師
title_i18n.en: P1 Assembly Challenge: Core Particles in Action

context_i18n.zh_tw: 情境：被問到『誰是工程師？』，你指名自己。
context_i18n.en: Context: Asked 'Who is the engineer?', you point to yourself.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 請組出：是我，我是工程師。
prompt_i18n.en: Assemble: It's me, I am the engineer.

context_i18n.zh_tw: 情境：向新認識的人簡單介紹自己的職業。
context_i18n.en: Context: Introducing your job to a new acquaintance.

context_i18n.zh_tw: 情境：單純描述你在『喝咖啡』這件事。
context_i18n.en: Context: Simply describing the action of 'drinking coffee'.

context_i18n.zh_tw: 情境：問某人是否在『吃飯』。
context_i18n.en: Context: Asking someone if they are 'eating a meal'.

prompt_i18n.en: Assemble: Are you eating a meal?

context_i18n.zh_tw: 情境：強調你不吃別的，『(至於) 麵包』倒是吃的。
context_i18n.en: Context: Emphasizing you don't eat other things, but as for bread... you do eat it.

prompt_i18n.en: Assemble: (As for) bread, I eat it.

title_i18n.zh_tw: P2 快閃記憶：核心助詞精準反應

answer_i18n.en: Emphasizes: I am the one who will do it.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【用法筆記】當情境是在『指名主角』或回答『誰是...』時，用 이/가。注意 저 + 가 必須變更為 제가。
note_i18n.en: [Usage Note] Use 이/가 when spotlighting the subject or answering 'Who is...'. Note the special form: 저 + 가 = 제가.

prompt_i18n.zh_tw: 這句通常用在什麼時候？

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 用途：一般介紹話題或表達對比。
answer_i18n.en: Usage: General topic intro or expressing contrast.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【語感筆記】은/는 是『拋錨』帶入話題。這句話聽起來像是在介紹自己的情況，或者在跟別人對比（別人不做，至於我，我做）。
note_i18n.en: [Nuance Note] 은/는 acts as an 'anchor' to introduce a topic. This sounds like you're either introducing your own situation or contrasting yourself with others.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【語法筆記】을/를 是受詞助詞，標示動作（吃）的對象。如果前面有名詞結尾是收音，接 을。
note_i18n.en: [Grammar Note] 을/를 is the object marker, marking the target of the action (eating). Use 을 if the preceding noun ends in a consonant (batchim).

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 含意：至於飯(對比)，我是吃的。
answer_i18n.en: Nuance: As for the rice/meal (contrast), I'll eat it.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【對比筆記】當 을/를 被換成 은/는 時，強烈的『對比感』就出來了。暗示：別的我可能不吃，但『飯』我是吃的。
note_i18n.en: [Contrast Note] Replacing 을/를 with 은/는 creates a strong sense of 'contrast'. It implies: I might not eat other things, but I'll eat THE MEAL.

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 焦點：強調這顆蘋果很好吃。
answer_i18n.en: Focus: Specifically focusing on 'the apple' being the tasty one.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【自然度筆記】在描述當下看到的特定對象（如：剛吃一口蘋果）的狀態時，常用 이/가 來標示當下的主語焦點。
note_i18n.en: [Naturalness Note] Use 이/가 when focusing on a specific subject in the immediate context (e.g., describing the apple you just bit into).

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 焦點：一般描述「蘋果」或對比。
answer_i18n.en: Focus: General description or contrast regarding apples.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【含義筆記】這可以用在一般性的陳述（蘋果很好吃），或者情境對比（西瓜不好吃，但『蘋果』很好吃）。
note_i18n.en: [Meaning Note] This can be used for general statements (Apples are tasty) or contextual contrast (Watermelons are bad, but APPLES are good).

answer_i18n.en: Answer: 을/를. No batchim, so use 를.

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【拼字筆記】커피 (Coffee) 以母音結尾，所以接 를。請記住：動作對象的預設標籤就是 을/를。
note_i18n.en: [Spelling Note] 커피 ends in a vowel, so attach 를. Remember: 을/를 is the default tag for action targets.

answer_i18n.zh_tw: 答案：不正確。一定要變身。

note_i18n.zh_tw: 【特例警告】韓文中有幾個詞接 가 會變形。저 + 가 = 제가。絕對不能說『저가』，這在韓文中極度不自然。
note_i18n.en: [Special Warning] A few words transform when met with 가. 저 + 가 = 제가. Saying '저가' is extremely unnatural in Korean.

title_i18n.zh_tw: R1 課程總結：三大助詞大哉問
title_i18n.en: R1 Lesson Wrap-up: Mastered the Trio?

prompts_i18n.zh_tw: 自我介紹（新話題）時，『我』接哪個助詞？
prompts_i18n.en: For self-intros (new topic), which particle for 'I'?

prompts_i18n.zh_tw: 強調『指名就是那個人』時，接哪組助詞？
prompts_i18n.en: Which set of particles for 'spotlighting' a specific subject?

prompts_i18n.zh_tw: 標示動作的『受詞目標』時，用哪組助詞？
prompts_i18n.en: Which set of particles marks the 'object target' of an action?
